description: Figure 3 presents inverse pole figures (IPFs) showing the texture evolution of austenite along the tensile axis (TA) in Fe-35Mn-6Al-0.1C low-carbon low-density steel during deformation at room temperature (RT) and liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT). The maps display the progression from weak initial texture (Max=2.55 at 0% strain) to strong <111>//TA fiber texture with increasing tensile strain. At RT, the texture strength gradually increases to Max=4.81 at 44% strain, while at LNT, the texture develops more intensely, reaching Max=6.68 at 55% strain. The color-coded orientation distribution maps reveal that cryogenic deformation promotes stronger <111> alignment parallel to the tensile axis compared to room temperature deformation. This enhanced texture intensity at LNT correlates with the higher uniform elongation (56.0% vs 38.5%) and extensive deformation twinning observed at cryogenic temperatures, supporting the relationship between texture development and superior mechanical properties during low-temperature deformation.
